title: Deep ProteoSphere literature hunt for Tier 1 biomolecular interaction dataset failures
discovery_queries: "protein-protein interaction" train test leakage dataset github
"The Davis and KIBA data can be downloaded from https://github.com/thinng/GraphDTA/tree/master"

query_log.why_it_helped: It surfaced benchmark inheritance from DeepDTA/GraphDTA repositories and code.

query_log.query: "The Davis and KIBA data can be downloaded from https://github.com/thinng/GraphDTA/tree/master"
query_log.why_it_helped: It found later journal papers that explicitly inherit GraphDTA benchmark artifacts without a cold split.
It focused the search on papers whose headline evaluation still used the classic core-set benchmark family.
It surfaced mitigation-aware baselines such as PotentialNet and helped prevent over-claiming.

tier1_candidates.title: Struct2Graph: a graph attention network for structure based predictions of protein-protein interactions
tier1_candidates.suspected_mitigation_strategy: none surfaced in released split logic
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: ProteoSphere already reproduced the released split logic and found 643 shared PDB IDs between train and test.

tier1_candidates.title: An artificial neural network model to predict structure-based protein-protein free energy of binding from Rosetta-calculated properties
tier1_candidates.suspected_mitigation_strategy: none validated on recovered benchmark/external rosters
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: ProteoSphere recovered the benchmark pool and three validation panels; all three external panels fail independence checks.

tier1_candidates.title: DeepDTA: deep drug-target binding affinity prediction
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: DeepDTA ships `train_fold_setting1.txt` and `test_fold_setting1.txt`; ProteoSphere re-computed overlap directly from the official files.

tier1_candidates.title: GraphDTA: predicting drug-target binding affinity with graph neural networks
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official GraphDTA README says the Davis/KIBA `test_fold_setting1.txt` and `train_fold_setting1.txt` files were downloaded from DeepDTA.

tier1_candidates.title: GANsDTA: Predicting Drug-Target Binding Affinity Using GANs
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official Frontiers article states that the Davis and KIBA experiments used the same setting as DeepDTA, with 80% training and 20% testing.

tier1_candidates.title: CSatDTA: Prediction of Drug-Target Binding Affinity Using Convolution Model with Self-Attention
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The vendored data README exposes `test_fold_setting1.txt` and `train_fold_setting1.txt` and points back to the DeepDTA data article.

tier1_candidates.title: iEdgeDTA: integrated edge information and 1D graph convolutional neural networks for binding affinity prediction
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official iEdgeDTA code loads `original/folds/train_fold_setting1.txt` and `original/folds/test_fold_setting1.txt`, and the README points back to DeepDTA for training-dataset information.

tier1_candidates.title: SAG-DTA: Prediction of Drug-Target Affinity Using Self-Attention Graph Network
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official data-preparation script says `convert data from DeepDTA` and reads `train_fold_setting1.txt` plus `test_fold_setting1.txt`.

tier1_candidates.title: EMPDTA: An End-to-End Multimodal Representation Learning Framework with Pocket Online Detection for Drug-Target Affinity Prediction
tier1_candidates.suspected_mitigation_strategy: none for the headline Davis/KIBA results
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official README states that the sequence-based datasets and the split came from DeepDTA and MDeePred.

tier1_candidates.title: DGDTA: dynamic graph attention network for predicting drug-target binding affinity
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official article says the Davis and KIBA data can be downloaded from the GraphDTA repository, which inherits the DeepDTA setting1 split.

tier1_candidates.title: GS-DTA: integrating graph and sequence models for predicting drug-target binding affinity
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official article says the Davis and KIBA data can be downloaded from the GraphDTA repository.

tier1_candidates.title: MEGDTA: multi-modal drug-target affinity prediction based on protein three-dimensional structure and ensemble graph neural network
tier1_candidates.suspected_mitigation_strategy: none for the headline Davis/KIBA evaluation
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official article says the Davis and KIBA datasets can be downloaded from the GraphDTA repository.

tier1_candidates.title: Development and evaluation of a deep learning model for protein-ligand binding affinity prediction
tier1_candidates.suspected_mitigation_strategy: no homology-aware split surfaced in headline benchmark
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The paper's widely reused benchmark story is training on PDBbind and evaluating on CASF/core-set scoring power; ProteoSphere's direct overlap audit shows the core-set family is not independent at the protein level.

tier1_candidates.title: OnionNet: a Multiple-Layer Intermolecular-Contact-Based Convolutional Neural Network for Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Prediction
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official OnionNet README says the testing set is the CASF-2013 benchmark and the PDBbind v2016 core set.

tier1_candidates.title: SE-OnionNet: A Convolution Neural Network for Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Prediction
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official Frontiers article says the model was tested using scoring functions on PDBbind and the CASF-2016 benchmark.

tier1_candidates.title: OnionNet-2: A Convolutional Neural Network Model for Predicting Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Based on Residue-Atom Contacting Shells
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official Frontiers article says OnionNet-2 was trained on the PDBbind database and evaluated primarily on CASF-2016.

tier1_candidates.title: SS-GNN: A Simple-Structured Graph Neural Network for Affinity Prediction
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official paper describes results on the standard PDBbind v2016 core test set without a homology- or time-based mitigation layer.

tier1_candidates.title: GNNSeq: A Sequence-Based Graph Neural Network for Predicting Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The paper reports benchmark performance on the PDBbind refined/core lineage without an orthogonal cold-target or homology-aware split.

tier1_candidates.title: CurvAGN: curvatures-based Adaptive Graph Neural Network for protein-ligand binding affinity prediction
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official article says the model was trained on the standard PDBbind-v2016 dataset and evaluated on the PDBbind v2016 core set.

tier1_candidates.title: DeepTGIN: improving protein-ligand affinity prediction with a hybrid temporal and graph interaction network
tier1_candidates.evidence_note: The official article says DeepTGIN uses the PDBbind 2016 core set as the primary test set and the PDBbind 2013 core set as an additional test set.

tier2_candidates.reason: Strong PPIS paper that still lives inside the Train_335/Test_60 benchmark family rather than proving out-of-family generalization.
Useful PPIS benchmark paper, but not a hard failure because exact chain-level overlap was not mirrored and the issue is benchmark saturation rather than proven leakage.
Another strong within-family PPIS benchmark paper that is better framed as a supporting saturation case than a Tier 1 failure.
Shares the same benchmark-family dependence story as other late PPIS papers in the Train_335 lineage.
Good supporting example of repeated benchmark-family reuse without enough out-of-family validation.

control_candidates.reason_for_control: Strict C1/C2/C3 split design and explicit release artifacts make this a good fairness check for the analyzer.
Benchmark anchor for the PPIS family; useful as a control because it is paper-faithful and the main concern is later family saturation, not a proven split failure here.

control_candidates.title: BatchDTA: implicit batch alignment enhances deep learning-based drug-target affinity estimation
control_candidates.reason_for_control: The official README says the Davis/KIBA benchmark is split based on unseen protein sequence, which directly addresses the warm-start failure seen in DeepDTA-style setting1.

control_candidates.title: Hierarchical graph representation learning for the prediction of drug-target binding affinity
control_candidates.reason_for_control: The official repository exposes S1/S2/S3/S4 training and testing settings, which is mitigation-aware rather than a single warm-start benchmark.

control_candidates.title: NHGNN-DTA: a node-adaptive hybrid graph neural network for interpretable drug-target binding affinity prediction
control_candidates.reason_for_control: The released split utility explicitly implements cold-target, cold-drug, and cold target+drug settings.

control_candidates.title: PotentialNet for Molecular Property Prediction
control_candidates.reason_for_control: PotentialNet explicitly proposes sequence- and structure-homology-clustered cross-validation to measure generalizability more honestly.

control_candidates.title: Improved Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Prediction with Structure-Based Deep Fusion Inference
control_candidates.journal: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
control_candidates.reason_for_control: The paper keeps the standard core-set comparison for literature continuity but also adds a temporal plus 3D structure-clustered holdout for novel protein targets.

backlog_candidates.title: AttentionDTA: drug-target binding affinity prediction by sequence-based deep learning with attention mechanism
backlog_candidates.journal: IEEE/ACM Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
backlog_candidates.reason: The repository exposes the datasets but not enough split provenance to prove whether the paper inherits a warm-start split or uses something colder.

backlog_candidates.title: NerLTR-DTA: drug-target binding affinity prediction based on neighbor relationship and learning to rank
backlog_candidates.reason: The current repo and landing-page evidence are not yet strong enough to prove which split family underlies the headline results.

backlog_candidates.title: Predicting Drug-Target Affinity by Learning Protein Knowledge From Biological Networks
backlog_candidates.journal: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
backlog_candidates.reason: The paper is interesting, but the public evidence located in this run is still too weak to prove whether the headline split is warm-start or mitigation-aware.
